conclusions.fixed_order_nine: every consecutive-row arbitrary-column reshaped-Hankel minor through order nine has its required sign on -100<=lambda<=0
conclusions.next_handoff: derive a uniform-in-order endpoint and forward mechanism; fixed order nine alone is not PF-infinity

proof_boundary: This artifact proves the required signed Hankel layers through fixed order nine only. It does not prove order ten or higher, PF-infinity, the all-degree Jensen bridge, RH, or Lambda<=0.

rows.claim: Every signed order-nine endpoint coordinate is strictly positive at lambda=-100.
Signed order-nine positivity is uniform at sufficiently large shift throughout the compact heat interval.
rows.formula: there exists N_9 such that Q_(9,n)(lambda)>0 for every n>=N_9 and -100<=lambda<=0

rows.claim: The signed order-nine layer obeys a nearest-neighbor cooperative system with positive forward coefficient.
rows.proof_boundary: Exact Plucker and heat-flow algebra inside the completed order-eight cone.

rows.claim: The uniform tail confines any first loss to finitely many shifts, where variation of constants preserves positivity.
rows.proof_boundary: Infinite-index maximum principle with compact-uniform tail.

rows.claim: Endpoint entry, the uniform tail, and the cooperative system prove signed contiguous order nine throughout the interval.
rows.proof_boundary: All shifts and all finite heat parameters from -100 through zero.

rows.claim: The completed lower contiguous layers supply every initial-minor sign through order eight.
rows.formula: epsilon_k*H_(k,n)(lambda)>0 for k=1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8, all n and -100<=lambda<=0

rows.claim: The fixed-order initial-minor criterion transfers signed contiguous order nine to all arbitrary column sets.
rows.proof_boundary: Finite-dimensional transfer applied pointwise in lambda.

rows.claim: Fixed order nine does not supply the all-order theorem required by the Jensen bridge.
rows.formula: derive a uniform-in-order endpoint and forward mechanism; fixed order nine alone is not PF-infinity
rows.proof_boundary: Open uniform-in-order problem; not PF-infinity, RH, or Lambda<=0.

source_contract.conditional_arbitrary_columns: completed signed contiguous layers through order nine imply every arbitrary-column signed layer through order nine
source_contract.conditional_forward: [Q_(9,n)(-100)>0 for all n] => [Q_(9,n)(lambda)>0 for all n and -100<=lambda<=0]
source_contract.fixed_order_transfer: [epsilon_k H_(k,s)>0 for 1<=k<=m, all s] => [epsilon_k R_(k,n)(j_1,...,j_k)>0 for 1<=k<=m]
source_contract.lower_layers: epsilon_k*H_(k,n)(lambda)>0 for k=1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8, all n and -100<=lambda<=0

sources.status: rigorous all-shift signed order-nine entry at lambda=-100
exact uniform eventual signed order-nine tail and conditional forward reduction with one open lambda=-100 entry
signed contiguous and arbitrary-column order-eight theorem on -100<=lambda<=0
exact arbitrary-column reshaped-Hankel order-four theorem at lambda zero

source_contract.uniform_tail: there exists N_9 such that Q_(9,n)(lambda)>0 for every n>=N_9 and -100<=lambda<=0

status: signed contiguous and arbitrary-column order-nine theorem on -100<=lambda<=0
